title: SieveErrorKind::Lex vs Parse distinguished by line.is_some() — fragile invariant
description: SCENARIO: parse_error.rs From<ParseError> for SieveError uses 'if e.line.is_some()' to decide whether the error came from the lexer (SieveErrorKind::Lex) or the form parser (SieveErrorKind::Parse). This invariant is stated only in a comment: 'the lexer always sets line/col; the form parser never does'. If any future code path in the form parser sets line/col (e.g. when adding position tracking to the form layer), the Lex/Parse classification silently misclassifies. MAINTENANCE CONSEQUENCE: callers matching SieveErrorKind::Lex vs ::Parse get wrong results without any compile-time or test-time signal. Fix: add a discriminant field to ParseError (e.g. an enum or bool 'from_lexer') or split ParseError into two types so the distinction is structural rather than a naming convention encoded in an Option.
